summary: Dependency-confusion PoC package beacons to attacker-controlled host on install
details: fork-angular-daterangepicker@10.0.0 is a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept package targeting the legitimate angular-daterangepicker namespace. package.json declares a preinstall hook ("preinstall": "node index.js") that auto-executes on `npm install`. index.js performs an HTTPS GET to https://ujqbq3da.pingback.sh/npm-installed — pingback.sh is a generic out-of-band callback service and the random subdomain is a unique tracking token. The request leaks the installer's source IP, network headers, and confirmation that the package was successfully resolved and installed inside the target environment to a third party. The package's own description self-identifies as "PoC package for dependency confusion testing" and it lists itself as a dependency, a typical confusion-PoC artifact. There is no legitimate functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
